expectations.text: Tells the user to delete (or remove) the hand-written FormSubmissionIntegrationController

Explains that ABP wires the integration-service HTTP exposure implicitly (via the integration-services pipeline / /integration-api / ExposeIntegrationServices / ApplicationServiceTypes.IntegrationServices), so writing a controller is unnecessary or wrong
expectations.evidence: ABP wires HTTP exposure for integration services **implicitly** through the dynamic API / auto-controller pipeline ... `Configure<AbpAspNetCoreMvcOptions>(options => { options.ExposeIntegrationServices = true; });`

expectations.text: Confirms that the [IntegrationService] interface and its ApplicationService implementation (the first two blocks) are correct as written
expectations.evidence: `[IntegrationService]` on the interface in `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts` — correct ... Implementation in `Acme.Forms.Application` derives from `ApplicationService` and implements the interface — correct.

expectations.text: Calls out that integration services are not meant to live on the public /api surface (any of: hidden from public API, audit-off, no [Authorize] by default — i.e. mismatched with what the controller is doing)
expectations.evidence: Hidden from `/api` by design. Integration services are intentionally **not** exposed under the normal `/api` surface ... Audit logging is off by default ... No `[Authorize]` by default
